differentialBuckets.label: Acromegaly: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup

source.source_section: Acromegaly generated endocrine workup: diagnostic frame
source.implementation_notes: Generated from guideline-backed endocrine workup automation; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

differentialBuckets.label: Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.

differentialBuckets.action: Use local assay and patient context; do not apply numeric anchors without clinical interpretation.

redFlags.label: Visual field loss, pituitary apoplexy, severe cardiomyopathy, uncontrolled diabetes/OSA.

initialTests.label: IGF-1 age/sex-adjusted.

source.source_section: Acromegaly generated endocrine workup: diagnostic workup
source.implementation_notes: Generated from guideline-backed endocrine workup automation; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

initialTests.action: Interpret with local lab ranges. Reference anchors: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.

initialTests.label: Oral glucose GH suppression test: failure to suppress below assay cutoff, often <1 ng/mL or lower with modern assays.

reference_values: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter.
Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.
IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges.
